title: rubus glabricaulis
intro: Rubus glabricaulis, also known as the hairy-leaved bramble, is a species of bramble native to China and parts of Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. Rubus glabricaulis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction for its medicinal uses, and its leaves and stems are also used in traditional Chinese medicine.
